title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn requests that Taylor open a sealed cabinet in her upstairs bedroom. After a night of feeling uneasy and fearful in the house, she opens the cabinet the following day and discovers that the floral wallpaper preserved inside is different from the room's current wallpaper. Disturbed because the pattern inside the cabinet is identical to one she had recently imagined for the room, Gwendolyn wonders if she is experiencing temporal anomalies or seeing the past, referencing the theories of John William Dunne. Seeking clarity, she sends a telegram to her cousin Raymond West in Chelsea, requesting a visit.

Upon arriving in Chelsea, Gwendolyn feels nervous around Raymond and his wife, Joan, due to their elite cultural discourse and unusual mannerisms. Raymond presents a schedule of cultural events and introduces Gwendolyn to Aunt Jane (Miss Maple), a woman known for her interest in solving puzzles and her involvement in a previous murder case. Gwendolyn meets the elegant Miss Maple and later attends the Majesty Theatre with Raymond, Joan, an artist, and a lawyer. During the performance, a terrifying climax involving a character's death causes Gwendolyn to scream and flee the theater in a panic. She runs blindly toward the Strand and eventually hails a taxi in Piccadilly to return to the house in Chelsea, arriving in a state of visible trembling.
